原文: Is Fresh Air Really Good for You? We all grew up hearing people tell us to "go out and get some fresh air." E. But is fresh air really as good for you as your mother always said? According to recent studies①, the answer is a big YES, if the air quality② in your camping area is good.
翻译: 新鲜空气真的对你有益吗？我们从小都听人们告诉我们“出去呼吸点新鲜空气”。但是，新鲜空气真的像你母亲常说的那样对你有益吗？根据最近的研究，答案是肯定的，前提是你露营区域的空气质量良好。
必背词汇:
① recent /ˈriːsnt/ adj. 最近的；近来的
Ⓡ recently /ˈriːsntli/ adv. 最近
Ⓡ recentness /ˈriːsntnəs/ n. 新近
Ⓡ ancient /ˈeɪnʃənt/ adj. 古代的
Ⓡ current /ˈkʌrənt/ adj. 当前的
② quality /ˈkwɒləti/ n. 质量；品质
Ⓡ quantity /ˈkwɒntəti/ n. 数量
Ⓡ qualify /ˈkwɒlɪfaɪ/ v. 使合格
Ⓡ qualification /ˌkwɒlɪfɪˈkeɪʃn/ n. 资格
Ⓡ qualitative /ˈkwɒlɪtətɪv/ adj. 定性的

原文: A. Fresh air cleans our lungs③. If the air you're breathing is clean—which it would be if you're away from the smog④ of cities—then the air is filled with life-giving, energizing oxygen⑤. If you exercise out of doors, your body will learn to breathe more deeply, allowing even more oxygen to get to your muscles(肌肉)and your brain.
翻译: A. 新鲜空气清洁我们的肺部。如果你呼吸的空气是干净的——当你远离城市的烟雾时就会如此——那么空气中就充满了赋予生命、提供能量的氧气。如果你在户外锻炼，你的身体会学会更深地呼吸，让更多的氧气到达你的肌肉和大脑。
必背词汇:
③ lung /lʌŋ/ n. 肺
Ⓡ breathe /briːð/ v. 呼吸
Ⓡ breath /breθ/ n. 呼吸
Ⓡ respiratory /rəˈspɪrətri/ adj. 呼吸的
Ⓡ asthma /ˈæsmə/ n. 哮喘
④ smog /smɒɡ/ n. 烟雾
Ⓡ smoke /sməʊk/ n. 烟 v. 吸烟
Ⓡ fog /fɒɡ/ n. 雾
Ⓡ pollution /pəˈluːʃn/ n. 污染
Ⓡ pollutant /pəˈluːtənt/ n. 污染物
⑤ oxygen /ˈɒksɪdʒən/ n. 氧气
Ⓡ hydrogen /ˈhaɪdrədʒən/ n. 氢气
Ⓡ nitrogen /ˈnaɪtrədʒən/ n. 氮气
Ⓡ carbon dioxide /ˌkɑːbən daɪˈɒksaɪd/ n. 二氧化碳
Ⓡ atmosphere /ˈætməsfɪə(r)/ n. 大气

原文: Recently, people have begun studying the connection⑥ between the natural world and healing(治愈). G. All across the country, recovery centers have begun building Healing Gardens. In these places patients can go to be near nature during their recovery. It turns out that just looking at green, growing things can reduce stress⑦, lower blood pressure⑧, and put people into a better mood(情绪).Greenery is good for us. Hospital patients who see tree branches out their window are likely to recover at a faster rate than patients who see buildings or sky instead. C. Being in nature refreshes us. It gives us a great feeling of peace.
翻译: 最近，人们开始研究自然界与治愈之间的联系。G. 全国各地的康复中心已经开始建造治愈花园。在这些地方，患者可以在康复期间亲近自然。事实证明，仅仅看着绿色、生长的事物就能减轻压力、降低血压，并让人们情绪更好。绿色植物对我们有益。能看到窗外树枝的住院患者可能比看到建筑物或天空的患者恢复得更快。C. 身处大自然使我们精神焕发。它给我们一种极大的平静感。
必背词汇:
⑥ connection /kəˈnekʃn/ n. 联系；连接
Ⓡ connect /kəˈnekt/ v. 连接；联系
Ⓡ disconnect /ˌdɪskəˈnekt/ v. 断开连接
Ⓡ connective /kəˈnektɪv/ adj. 连接的
Ⓡ network /ˈnetwɜːk/ n. 网络
⑦ stress /stres/ n. 压力；强调 v. 强调
Ⓡ stressful /ˈstresfl/ adj. 压力大的
Ⓡ distress /dɪˈstres/ n. 痛苦；危难
Ⓡ pressure /ˈpreʃə(r)/ n. 压力
Ⓡ anxiety /æŋˈzaɪəti/ n. 焦虑
⑧ pressure /ˈpreʃə(r)/ n. 压力
Ⓡ press /pres/ v. 按；压 n. 新闻界
Ⓡ compress /kəmˈpres/ v. 压缩
Ⓡ depress /dɪˈpres/ v. 使沮丧
Ⓡ depression /dɪˈpreʃn/ n. 沮丧；萧条

原文: D. Another side benefit⑨ of getting fresh air is sunlight. While the sun's rays can age⑩ and harm our skin, they also give us beneficial vitamin D. To make sure you get enough vitamin D—but still protect your skin—put on sunscreen⑪ right as you head outside. It takes sunscreen about fifteen minutes to start working, and that's plenty of time for your skin to absorb⑫ a day's worth of vitamin D.
翻译: D. 呼吸新鲜空气的另一个附带好处是阳光。虽然太阳光线会使我们的皮肤老化并造成伤害，但它们也给我们提供有益的维生素D。为了确保你获得足够的维生素D——同时仍然保护你的皮肤——就在你出门时涂上防晒霜。防晒霜大约需要十五分钟才开始起作用，这段时间足够你的皮肤吸收一天所需的维生素D。
必背词汇:
⑨ benefit /ˈbenɪfɪt/ n. 好处；益处 v. 有益于
Ⓡ beneficial /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃl/ adj. 有益的
Ⓡ beneficiary /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃəri/ n. 受益人
Ⓡ profit /ˈprɒfɪt/ n. 利润
Ⓡ advantage /ədˈvɑːntɪdʒ/ n. 优势
⑩ age /eɪdʒ/ v. (使)变老 n. 年龄
Ⓡ aged /eɪdʒd/ adj. 年老的
Ⓡ aging /ˈeɪdʒɪŋ/ n. 老化
Ⓡ elderly /ˈeldəli/ adj. 年长的
Ⓡ youth /juːθ/ n. 青年时期
⑪ sunscreen /ˈsʌnskriːn/ n. 防晒霜
Ⓡ sunblock /ˈsʌnblɒk/ n. 防晒霜
Ⓡ sunburn /ˈsʌnbɜːn/ n. 晒伤
Ⓡ suntan /ˈsʌntæn/ n. 晒黑
Ⓡ lotion /ˈləʊʃn/ n. 护肤液
⑫ absorb /əbˈzɔːb/ v. 吸收；理解
Ⓡ absorption /əbˈzɔːpʃn/ n. 吸收
Ⓡ absorbent /əbˈzɔːbənt/ adj. 能吸收的
Ⓡ soak /səʊk/ v. 浸泡
Ⓡ digest /daɪˈdʒest/ v. 消化
